title: Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary, located in the Chincholi taluka of kalaburagi district of Karnataka, is the first drylands wildlife sanctuary in South India.
overview.description: Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary, located in the Chincholi taluka of kalaburagi district of Karnataka, is the first drylands wildlife sanctuary in South India. Spanning an area of 134.88 square kilometers, it is known for its rich biodiversity and unique ecosystem. The sanctuary features dry and moist deciduous forests, with plantations of teak, acacia, sandalwood, and red sanders. It also hosts medicinal herbs and trees.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (103 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (106 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chincholi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Inoli, Chincholi.
